Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 16:01 ET
P/C divergence signals institutional hedge into PCE with negative $11.4B gamma
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. Index puts heavy (2.52 P/C) while single-names see call dominance (0.88 P/C)
  • 2. Negative $11.4B SPY gamma amplifies moves with PCE Thursday catalyst pending
  • 3. ARM call surge +260% despite death cross — vol backwardation pricing event risk
NARRATIVE — defensive hedge into PCE

Institutional hedging patterns have crystallized ahead of Thursday's Core PCE print, with index put/call ratios spiking to 2.52 while single-name ratios remain call-heavy at 0.88 — a classic dispersion setup where portfolio protection coincides with individual stock bullishness. This positioning occurs against a fragmented sentiment backdrop where CNN Fear & Greed sits at Extreme Fear (15) yet options flow composite reads +31, creating a 63-point regime divergence. The VIX term structure remains in steep backwardation (-0.26 spread) while spot volatility holds elevated at 26.6, confirming near-term event risk is richly priced. Negative gamma positioning has reached extreme levels with SPY dealer exposure at -$11.4B, meaning any PCE-driven catalyst will trigger mechanical amplification in both directions as dealers hedge dynamically. This negative gamma environment makes the massive IWM put blocks particularly significant — 50,000 Mar 27 243P and 25,000 Mar 27 240P contracts suggest institutional protection extends beyond large caps into small-cap territory. The technical backdrop supports this defensive posture, with mega-cap death crosses in MSFT (score 15.7) and META (19.7) contrasting sharply with energy strength in XLE (score 89.5). ARM presents the day's most intriguing contradiction, generating +260% call flow surge to a 0.34 P/C ratio despite sitting in technical death cross territory. The stock trades in vol backwardation with IV elevated 67% above its 20-day average, suggesting option buyers are paying up for imminent catalyst protection. This mirrors the broader market dynamic where defensive positioning meets selective bullish bets, particularly in semiconductor names where Millennium's Q4 conviction builds in NVDA (+93%) align with today's 6,999 NVDA Apr 10 190C block trade.

DESKUS Today's Top News9:29 AM ET
________________________________ Synopsis (last modified 08:00 ET on 24-Mar-26) S&P futures lower: * S&P futures down 0.1% in Tuesday morning trading after US equities rallied to start the week, reversing overnight losses. All 11 S&P 500 sectors finished higher with more than 80% of the index up for the day. Small caps, travel/tourism, homebuilders, metals, machinery, rare earths and quantum computing some of the notable outperformers. Memory one of the higher-profile laggards. Treasuries weaker
________________________________ Synopsis (last modified 08:00 ET on 24-Mar-26) S&P futures lower: * S&P futures down 0.1% in Tuesday morning trading after US equities rallied to start the week, reversing overnight losses. All 11 S&P 500 sectors finished higher with more than 80% of the index up for the day. Small caps, travel/tourism, homebuilders, metals, machinery, rare earths and quantum computing some of the notable outperformers. Memory one of the higher-profile laggards. Treasuries weaker with yields up 3-4 bp. Dollar index up 0.3%. Gold little changed. Silver up 1.2%. Bitcoin futures up 0.3%. WTI crude up 3.2%. Brent up 2.7%. * Quieter from a headline perspective. Iran continues to largely deny ceasefire negotiations with the US but not necessarily surprising and the stream of backchannel talk headlines suggest something is going on. However, Strait of Hormuz still effectively closed, a report yesterday said there are at least a dozen Iranian mines in the Strait, Saudi Arabia and UAE reportedly considering joining war against Iran, US still sending thousands more troops to the region and Israel and Iran have fired more missiles today. Outside of geopolitics, private credit worries back in the headlines with APO sticking to its 5% withdrawal cap on its flagship fund despite receiving redemption requests for more than double that amount. Also some focus on stagflation signals from flash Eurozone PMIs for March. * Final Q4 nonfarm productivity and unit labor costs and flash PMIs for March on the US economic calendar this morning. PMIs expected to get even more attention than usual given they represent the first batch of data reflecting the Iran conflict. Treasury to sell $69B of 2-year notes. In addition, Governor Barr scheduled to speak this evening. February import/export price data out on Wednesday, initial claims the lone release for Thursday morning and final University of Michigan consumer sentiment (and inflation expectations) for March caps off the week on Friday. * APO said investors looked to pull 11.2% (~$1.6B) from its flagship private credit fund but it will stick to its 5% threshold. FSK cut to junk at Moody's. JEF higher on FT report Japan's SFMG considering takeover (though nothing imminent). GILD confirmed a deal to acquire autoimmune disease-focused biotech Ouro Medicines for $1,675M in upfront cash consideration and up to $500M in contingent milestone payments. EL confirmed reports that surfaced just before the close on Monday that it is in talks to acquire Spain's Puig. NTGR up big on Reuters report FCC banning the import of all new foreign-made consumer routers. BABA launched new chip for agentic IA and inference computing. SK Hynix considering ~$10B US listing. Top Stories (last modified 07:24 ET on 24-Mar-26) Morning Headlines: * Iran conflict: * Trump's reprieve offer came after US and Gulf allies warned about dangers of striking Iran's energy sites (link missiles into Israel, continues to dismiss Trump's negotiation claims to energy sites (Semafor<https://www.semafor.com/article/03/23/2026/us-to-keep-attacking-iran-as-israel-stays-on-sidelines-of-talks>) * Gulf states nearing decision to join fight against Iran following relentless missile and drone attacks (link<https://www.msn.com/en-us/news/world/gulf-states-edge-toward-joining-fight-against-iran/ar-AA1ZfGxa>, Bloomberg hope for in-person de-escalation talks with Iran (London Times <https://www.thetimes.com/us/news-today/article/witkoff-travels-to-pakistan-in-hope-of-in-person-talks-with-iran-pn732tdb5> , NBC News vehicle output at Kentucky, Indiana plants (Bloomberg<https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-23/toyota-to-invest-further-1-billion-to-boost-output-at-us-plants>) * Markets: * Traders made crude oil bets worth $580M roughly 15 minutes before Trump's post about talks with Iran sent prices tumbling (FT<https://www.ft.com/content/1171d623-3709-4f6e-8ded-a5df4ec57696>) * US equities perform better amid Iran war, but risks of severe declines remain if conflict continues (Reuters <https://www.reuters.com/business/iran-fallout-us-shares-hold-up-better-than-global-rivals-now-2026-03-24> ) * (Bloomberg<https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-24/ecb-must-be-vigilant-in-face-of-stagflation-risks-vujcic-says>) * RBNZ Governor Breman signals rate hike if inflation stays higher for longer (Reuters<https://www.reuters.com/world/asia-pacific/nz-central-bank-warns-rates-could-rise-should-energy-shock-prove-protracted-2026-03-23>) * Trade/Tariffs: * Senators urge US Commerce Department to suspend Nvidia's AI chip export licenses to China, Southeast Asia (FT <https://www.ft.com/content/556e534d-bbc5-46e0-8965-ec3a13a8871a?syn-25a6b1a6=1> ) * US congressional advisory body warns China's lower cost, open-source AI is giving it competitive advantage challenges in Iran, aiming to shift power balance (FT <https://www.ft.com/content/3f0b5309-2791-4370-a6d9-d0ed84820db5?syn-25a6b1a6=1> ) * Beijing forum pitches China as predictable economic anchor to routers, impacting China (Reuters<https://www.reuters.com/sustainability/boards-policy-regulation/fcc-banning-imports-new-chinese-made-routers-citing-security-concerns-2026-03-23>) * US Ambassador to Europe warns EU faces higher tariffs if parliament doesn't approve trade deal (Bloomberg<https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-23/us-envoy-warns-eu-can-expect-higher-tariffs-if-trade-deal-dies>, FT <https://www.ft.com/content/6bf153e4-11af-44d5-9d1c-48b5c7ad26ef?accessToken=zwAAAZ22FhyGkc9r8VPkEa9E1dOdHEi1x60m7wE.MEQCIGxsQoKLRN2PuwDwBjG3oNoXM345IuHdRF5UeuBk0ndkAiBVJqr4dPWIFpTspxWMChSEnhRKF-Q-H7AKJbEDvzVRSw&segmentId=7d4bcc2e-e664-92ba-62e3-5590579f1902&syn-25a6b1a6=1> ) * Washington: * Senate Republicans, after meeting Trump, claim to have a solution for DHS shutdown(Politico <https://www.politico.com/news/2026/03/23/dhs-shutdown-talks-breakthrough-00841195> , Axios<https://www.axios.com/2026/03/23/dhs-funding-house-senate-budget-talks-reconciliation>, link <https://www.msn.com/en-us/news/politics/senators-close-in-on-deal-to-end-dhs-shutdown-fund-tsa/ar-AA1ZeECu> ) * Supreme Court appears poised to limit counting of mail-in ballots arriving after Election Day (Washington Post<https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2026/03/23/supreme-court-mail-in-ballots-mississippi>) Tuesday morning check-in: * Quieter from a headline perspective. Iran continues to largely deny ceasefire negotiations with the US but not necessarily surprising and the stream of backchannel talk headlines over the last 24 hours or so suggest something is going on. * However, Strait of Hormuz still effectively closed, report yesterday said there are at least a dozen Iranian mines in the Strait, Saudi Arabia and UAE reportedly considering joining war against Iran, US still sending thousands more troops to the region and Israel and Iran have fired more missiles today. * Outside of geopolitics, private credit worries back in the headlines with Apollo sticking to its 5% withdrawal cap on its flagship fund despite receiving redemption requests for more than double that amount. In addition, FS KKR credit rating cut to junk at Moody's on concerns about asset quality. * Also some focus on stagflation signals from flash Eurozone PMIs for March. S&P Global flash composite PMI fell to 50.5 from 51.9 in prior month, below 51.0 consensus and weakest reading since last May. Input cost inflation accelerated to fastest pace in over three years. * Lot of uncertainty about what's next. Monday move flagged as more about short covering/hedge lifting (particularly at the ETF level) than any reset in risk appetite. At the same time, magnitude and duration of the drawdown continue to fit with historical geopolitical shock pattern (which has been a dip buying opportunity). Back-channel diplomacy may be taking place, but Iran war remains hot (and could pull in other regional powers): * Market still focused on trying to flesh out Trump's Monday claim of diplomacy with new Iranian leadership. No specifics from the White House and nothing further (yet) on Truth Social. Iranian sources have denied any direct talks and administration has said contacts at an early stage (NY Times<https://www.nytimes.com/2026/03/23/us/politics/trump-iran-gas-oil-strait.html>). However, a lot of references to possible indirect/back-channel contacts, with Omani, Egyptian, and Pakistani officials mentioned (CBS News<https://www.cbsnews.com/news/iran-us-message-mediators-trump-iran-war-possible-deal>, link<https://www.msn.com/en-us/news/world/the-back-channel-diplomacy-behind-trump-s-u-turn-on-iran/ar-AA1ZfXwS?ocid=weather-verthp-feeds>, AP<https://apnews.com/live/iran-war-israel-trump-03-24-2026#0000019d-1e91-d5b1-a19f-9effb3bf0000>). Note Bloomberg<https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-23/trump-s-market-driven-reversal-buys-time-but-skepticism-abounds?srnd=homepage-americas> reported yesterday's de-escalatory tone came after regional allies warned Trump the war was becoming a disaster. * Meanwhile, conflict continues across the region, with Iran launching multiple strikes at Israel, US Mideast bases, and other regional powers; Israel has continued pressing in Lebanon (Bloomberg<https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-24/iran-strikes-persist-even-as-trump-claims-talks-to-end-conflict>). US officials note there are now at least a dozen underwater mines in the Strait of Hormuz (CBS News<https://www.cbsnews.com/news/strait-of-hormuz-mines-iran-talks-officials/>). And a lot of focus on a media report<https://www.msn.com/en-us/news/world/gulf-states-edge-toward-joining-fight-against-iran/ar-AA1ZfGxa?cvid=7f2ca8be935149aeee24a11a71a333dc&ocid=BingNewsVerp> Saudi Arabia may be near entering the war while the UAE is beginning to crack down on Iran-owned assets and debating military involvement. * Ultimately, a fair bit of skepticism about Trump's diplomatic claims, with observers seeing a real possibility of Trump executing postponed strikes on Iranian electrical infrastructure by the end of the week. And note a Marine Expeditionary Unit (2.5K troops) is en route to the region, while senior military officials also consider deployment of elements of the 82nd Airborne (NY Times<https://www.nytimes.com/2026/03/23/us/politics/us-airborne-troops-iran.html>). * Possibility of diplomacy delivered a bit of relief for energy prices yesterday with WTI dropping more than 10%, though crude giving some of that back today. Among today's energy headlines, Bloomberg<https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-24/fees-to-ship-saudi-crude-from-yanbu-ease-as-more-tankers-arrive> reports more tankers arriving at Saudi Arabia's Red Sea port of Yanbu to pick up diverted flows. Indian buyers uncertain about buying Iranian cargoes despite US waivers (Bloomberg<https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-24/us-approved-iranian-barrels-find-a-cautious-reception-in-india>). And White House says it has not received pushback from US producers about ramping up domestic production (Politico<https://www.politico.com/news/2026/03/23/iran-oil-agen-production-energy-00841108>). Private credit continues to make headlines: * Apollo said that while investors looked to pull ~$1.6B from its Apollo Debt Solutions BDC, representing 11.2% of its net assets, it stuck to its 5% withdrawal cap. Highlighted need to balance interest of investors seeking liquidity with those who choose to stay invested. * FT<https://www.ft.com/content/d315cb1c-1e1e-479c-a6d4-b3a817fead3e?syn-25a6b1a6=1> said that based on its estimates, investors have submitted redemption requests worth $11.7B across more than half a dozen funds it tracks. Added that thus far, only 66% of withdrawals have been met, totaling $7.8B. * While retail redemptions and software exposure (AI disruption terminal value debate) have grabbed most of the attention, credit quality also in play. FS KKR credit rating cut to junk at Moody's. Flagged continued asset quality challenges. * Sell-side research continues to paint a much less dire scenario than the financial press. Goldman Sachs said private credit stress is unlikely to generate large macroeconomic [...truncated to 2000 words]
OVI Scanner Context
MARKET CONTEXT (recent headlines): - [ft_markets] GB Energy chair calls for more North Sea oil production - [marketwatch] Oil prices rally as Pentagon reportedly plans to deploy 3,000 troops to the Middle East - [ft_markets] Oil rises back above $100 as Iran launches new strikes

ATM IV = at-the-money implied vol  |  IV PCTILE = where today's IV ranks vs ≈90 trading days (green=cheap, red=expensive)  |  Spread = ATM IV − RV 20d  |  Rich: IV/RV >1.2×  |  Skew 90/110: P(90%) − C(110%)  |  SKEW PCTILE: vs ≈90d history  |  PUT/CALL V%: daily volume rank vs ≈90d history  |  vs SECTOR: ticker ATM IV / sector ETF ATM IV (±10% threshold)  |  Divergence: cross-signal flags (VOL HIGH/SKEW FLAT, CHEAP VOL/RICH SKEW, etc.)
